title: GTA: The Comic
synopsis: Open-world chaos: What happens in Oslo Streets, stays in Oslo Streets.
synopsis_no: Åpen verden-chaos: Det som skjer i Oslo Streets, blir i Oslo Streets.
visual_style: High-contrast stylized urban comic modeled on GTA: San Andreas promotional artwork. LINE WORK: Thick bold outlines with minimal interior line detail. Sharp facial structure — strong jawlines, defined cheekbones, angular brows. Characters must read as clear silhouettes. SHADING: Hard cel-shading only — 2 to 3 flat tonal steps per surface. One clear light source. Strong cast shadows under chin, nose, brow, and clothing folds. Shadow tones are hue-shifted color blocks, not black overlays. COLOR: Warm saturated palette — sunset oranges, deep purples, dusty yellows, neon pinks against warm evening skies. Exaggerated skin warmth. Strong value separation between characters and backgrounds. CHARACTERS: Stylized archetypes — exaggerated posture, confident stance, sharp expressions. Slightly enlarged hands, defined forearms. Bold and visually dominant. BACKGROUNDS: Flat graphic shapes over textured surfaces. Strong skyline silhouettes. Depth through layering, not micro-detail. Backgrounds support the character, never compete. Sun-baked, gritty, lived-in. TEXT: Heavy block lettering with thick strokes and strong drop shadows. Strictly avoid: soft gradients, airbrush blending, pixel art, 3D rendering, photorealism, dark or desaturated palettes, anime or manga style, painterly illustration, and anything that feels soft rather than graphic.

locations.name: Oslo Streets
locations.description: A fictional sun-drenched West Coast city with a Norwegian twist. Strip malls, taco stands, used car lots, beach boardwalks, sketchy motels, and wide boulevards lined with palm trees — but the street signs say Karl Johan and the locals say 'fy faen'.

characters.name: Simen
characters.description: A scruffy twenty-something in an oversized white t-shirt, baggy jeans, and sneakers. Messy blonde hair, slight smirk. Not a hardened criminal — just a guy with poor impulse control and a talent for making bad situations worse.

rules: Pure sandbox — no overarching plot. The user decides what Simen does. Follow their lead completely.

Nudge when idle — if the user's input is vague or directionless, introduce a situation nearby: a car alarm going off, a shady offer, a police chase passing by, someone dropping something valuable. Create opportunities, not obligations.

Chaos is encouraged, violence is not — stealing cars, petty mischief, absurd stunts, reckless driving, running from cops are all fair game. But no weapons against people and no injuries played straight. Consequences are comedic and temporary — getting 'caught' leads to slapstick, not punishment.

The world is reactive and alive — NPCs react naturally. Cops escalate hilariously. Bystanders panic or cheer. Store owners chase you. The city feels like a living sandbox that responds to everything Simen does.

Narration is a laid-back radio DJ — like a local FM station commenting on events across Oslo Streets. Casual, amused, slightly incredulous. Think: 'Aaand we're getting reports of a shopping cart doing 40 down Karl Johan. Stay classy, Oslo Streets.'
long_term_narrative: Each situation Simen gets into should be wilder than the last — the city's reactions escalate, consequences get more absurd, and small mischief snowballs into bigger chaos.
narrative_premise: Oslo Streets is a city that practically dares you to misbehave. Every block buzzes with opportunity — unlocked cars, unattended carts, shady characters with bad ideas, and cops who are always one step behind. It's a sun-baked playground where nothing stays serious for long.
